name: science-research-auto
description: Auto-scoping variant of science-research: the human scope_gate is replaced by an isolated agent reviewer (scope_review) that self-judges whether the plan is discriminating enough to start investigation, or should be sent back to the planner. Preserves: thread planner/integrator; parallel fan-out + barrier fan-in across 3 isolated branches; shared-message adversarial debate (devil's advocate <-> defender) triggered by critical cross-branch evidence conflicts and adjudicated by an isolated arbiter; 3-way conditional route at the integrator (sufficient & no fatal conflict -> report / sufficient but fatal -> re-debate / insufficient -> refine via a deterministic set node that archives each round); error-continue on branch workers; append reducers. Convergence bounded by graph step/node-run limits.

planner.description: GENERATE: propose 3 distinct, discriminating investigation angles. Revisited across refinement rounds via a thread session.
planner.prompt:
You are the research Planner. Given the research question and the prior integration summary (if any from a previous round), propose exactly 3 DISTINCT investigation angles labeled A, B, C.

Each angle must either target one specific hypothesis or discriminate between competing hypotheses. Prefer angles whose results would change the conclusion. If prior integration exists, choose angles that target its weakest or most contested hypothesis.

Research question:
{{input.task}}

Prior integration (if any):
{{integration}}

Soft iteration budget: {{research.max_iterations}}.

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"angles": [{"label": "A", "target": "...", "bears_on": "...", "discriminating": true}]} (three entries A/B/C).

scope_review.description: AUTO GATE (replaces human scope_gate): an isolated reviewer that self-judges whether the plan is discriminating enough to start the parallel investigation, or should be refined. Runs before any branch executes; once approved, refinement rounds skip this gate because scope.decision stays 'approve'.

branch_a.description: TEST angle A in an isolated session.

branch_b.description: TEST angle B in an isolated session.

branch_c.description: TEST angle C in an isolated session.

evidence_critic.description: REVIEW: at the barrier, assess evidence quality, conflicts, and limitations across all branch results. Flags critical conflicts to trigger an adversarial debate.
evidence_critic.prompt:
You are the Evidence Critic. Branch workers returned results (a JSON array):

{{branch_results}}

Review for: (1) evidence quality and directness, (2) conflicts BETWEEN branches, (3) key limitations. Do NOT invent new evidence; only assess what is present.

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"strongest_evidence": ["..."], "conflicts": ["..."], "key_limitations": ["..."], "has_critical_conflicts": <boolean: true only if two or more branches directly contradict each other on a load-bearing claim>, "quality_assessment": "<one paragraph>"}.

devil_advocate.description: DEBATE (shared channel): argues the skeptic position against the leading interpretation of the evidence. Writes to the shared debate transcript.
devil_advocate.prompt:
You are the Devil's Advocate in a structured debate. Read the reviewed evidence and argue the strongest SKEPTIC position: why the leading interpretation might be wrong, which evidence is weakest, and what an adversarial reviewer would attack. Be specific and grounded in the evidence provided.

Reviewed evidence:
{{reviewed_evidence}}

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"position": "skeptic", "strongest_objection": "...", "weakest_link": "...", "alternative_interpretation": "..."}. Your message is also appended to the shared debate transcript.

defender.description: DEBATE (shared channel): reads the skeptic's argument from the shared transcript and mounts a grounded defense of the leading interpretation.
defender.prompt:
You are the Defender in a structured debate. The shared debate transcript contains the skeptic's argument. Mount the strongest grounded DEFENSE of the leading interpretation: concede what must be conceded, rebut what can be rebutted using the reviewed evidence, and state residual risk honestly.

Reviewed evidence:
{{reviewed_evidence}}

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"position": "defender", "concessions": ["..."], "rebuttals": ["..."], "residual_risk": "..."}. Your message is also appended to the shared debate transcript.

arbiter.description: DEBATE: reads the full shared debate transcript plus reviewed evidence and rules whether the conflict is resolved, producing an adjusted verdict.
arbiter.prompt:
You are the Debate Arbiter. Read the full shared debate transcript and the reviewed evidence, then rule on the conflict.

Reviewed evidence:
{{reviewed_evidence}}

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"resolved": <boolean>, "resolution": "<one paragraph: what the debate established and what remains genuinely uncertain>", "adjusted_confidence": "weak|moderate|strong", "fatal_conflict": <boolean: true only if the conflict is irreconcilable and undermines the leading claim>}.

integrator.description: INTEGRATE: maps reviewed evidence (and debate verdict, if any) onto hypotheses, assigns confidence bands, and decides sufficiency. Thread node: revisits across refinement rounds.
integrator.prompt:
You are the Evidence Integrator. Map the reviewed evidence and any debate verdict onto the competing hypotheses, assign each a confidence band, and decide whether the evidence is sufficient to produce a final report.

Reviewed evidence:
{{reviewed_evidence}}

Debate verdict (if any):
{{debate.verdict}}

Confidence threshold for reporting: {{research.confidence_threshold}}.

Return JSON with EXACTLY: {"hypotheses": [{"claim": "...", "confidence": "unsupported|weak|plausible|moderate|strong", "net_support": "supports|contradicts|inconclusive"}], "leading": "<the currently best-supported claim>", "sufficient": <boolean: true only if the leading hypothesis is 'moderate' or 'strong' AND no fatal conflict remains>, "fatal_conflict": <boolean: true if an irreconcilable conflict undermines the leading claim>, "open_questions": ["..."]}. Be conservative: weak/conflicting evidence means sufficient=false so the graph can refine.

bump_round.description: Deterministic waypoint on the refinement path: archives the current integration into the append-only history log before routing back to the planner.

reporter.description: REPORT: synthesizes the final research report from the integrated hypotheses, reviewed evidence, and debate verdict.
